summary: Sprint 16 â€” NEXUS PROTOCOL v2. minor.issue: Founder password hash in MongoDB didn't match 'Founder@KORE2026!' (hash from previous seed/test run). Fixed by direct DB reset during testing. Root cause: seed script may be re-hashing with different salt on each restart. Need consistent seeding.

passlib bcrypt warning: 'module bcrypt has no attribute __about__'. Cosmetic warning trapped by passlib â€” does NOT affect functionality but indicates version mismatch between passlib and bcrypt package.

ui_bugs.issue: Username 'STEFANO' (7 chars) still truncated to 'STEFA...' on KORE card â€” pre-existing issue from iteration 9

action_items: Fix founder password mismatch: ensure seed script (db_reset_legacy.py or startup seed) always sets a consistent bcrypt hash for the founder. The hash changes on each seed run due to bcrypt salting.
Fix username truncation on KORE card for 'STEFANO' (7 chars) â€” pre-existing issue from iteration 9
Optional: upgrade passlib>=1.7.5 or bcrypt>=4.0.0 to fix the __about__ attribute warning

Step2 anti-ghost VERIFIED: isScanning=false initially blocks all 17-point detection. Web fallback correctly sets isScanning=true after 3s. Footer shows 'NEXUS IS SEARCHING FOR ATHLETE...' + 'POSIZIONATI DAVANTI ALLA CAMERA' before scanning starts.
Step2 7-gate biometric flow VERIFIED: loading(1.8s)â†’positioning(isScanning guard)â†’verifyingâ†’pose_checkâ†’countdownâ†’beatsâ†’approved all wired correctly. No ghost-triggering observed.

Backend /api/nexus/5beat-dna VERIFIED: correctly saves all 6 KORE DNA keys (velocita/forza/resistenza/tecnica/mentalita/flessibilita), requires auth, validates dna_results, persists to user.dna, response includes status+dna_updated+user.
KORE tab DNA display VERIFIED: VEL:87, FOR:83, RES:91, TEC:88, MEN:94, FLE:79 all displayed correctly after 5-Beat scan sync. DNA_LABELS correctly maps mentalitaâ†’MEN and flessibilitaâ†’FLE.

rca_of_issue: FOUNDER PASSWORD MISMATCH: Root cause is bcrypt salted hashing â€” each call to hash_password() generates a different hash. If the founder was seeded with one hash but then the startup seed event (seed_data) re-runs without checking if founder already exists, or if any test creates a new hash, the stored hash diverges. The seed_data startup event only badges founders retroactively (is_founder=True) but does NOT reset passwords â€” so the mismatch must have come from a previous test run or manual password change. MITIGATION: Direct DB reset of password hash in this iteration. LONG-TERM FIX: Add idempotent seeding that only creates founder if not exists (already done for battles/disciplines/crews).
